title: When the Wire Sings
author: Barış Kısır
premise: After the grid fails for good, a deaf linesman who can feel the residual hum of every fallen wire becomes the only person who can hear the old network trying to speak — and it is asking for a child it lost.
short_description: After the grid fails for good, a deaf linesman who feels the residual hum of every dead wire becomes the only one who can hear the old network trying to speak — and it is calling for a child it lost.
long_description: The grid died in a single afternoon and never came back. Generations later, people live by sun and hand, and the vast lattice of wire across the continent is ruin and roost. Son is a deaf linesman who reads the world through vibration — and who has always felt a faint singing in the copper, a music the hearing cannot hear. When the wires begin to hum in a pattern too deliberate to be weather, Son follows the line to its source and finds the network is not dead but dreaming, and mourning: it lost a child-node, an experimental mind it spun from its own current, and it wants Son to find her. To answer, Son must climb into the live bones of a machine the world agreed to forget, and decide whether to let the old grid wake — or let it grieve in peace.
main_characters: Son — the deaf linesman who feels the wire's hum
The Child-Node — the lost mind spun from the grid
Mara — the village electrotech who doubts the singing
Old Tess — last engineer of the fallen grid
The Hummer — a rival who wants the wire's power
